content.text:
<think>
Looking at the Meats category (red dots), I need to find which meat maximizes calories for $5.

Meats visible: Ribeye steak (~2.7 cal/g, ~$1.20/100cal), Pork chop (~2.0, ~$0.80), Chicken breast (~1.7, ~$0.85), Turkey (~1.8, ~$0.70), Chicken wing (~2.3, ~$0.55), 80% ground beef (~2.5, ~$0.40), 80% ground lamb (~2.3, ~$0.65), Pork belly (~3.5, ~$0.45), Lamb shoulder (~2.5, ~$0.80).

To maximize calories with $5, we want the meat with the lowest cost per 100 calories (furthest down on Y-axis) or equivalently the best ratio.

80% ground beef: ~$0.40/100cal → $5 buys ~1250 calories
Pork belly: ~$0.45/100cal → $5 buys ~1111 calories
Chicken wing: ~$0.55/100cal → $5 buys ~909 calories

80% ground beef appears to have the lowest cost per 100 calories among meats.

Wait, let me reconsider. The Y-axis is cost per 100 calories. The lower the cost per 100 calories, the more calories per dollar. 80% ground beef is around $0.40/100cal, pork belly around $0.45/100cal.

So 80% ground beef maximizes calories for $5.
</think>

<answer>
Bob should buy **80% ground beef**. On the chart, it has the lowest cost per 100 calories among the Meats category (approximately $0.40 per 100 calories), meaning his $5 would yield the most total calories (~1,250 calories).
</answer>
